key_insights.insight: Chasing external validation and material success without inner alignment leads to unhappiness, even with outward achievements.
key_insights.quote: I was in the public eye you're making more money than ever and it was extraordinarily exciting but at the age of 48 you found yourself crying almost every day I was probably physically exhausted.I just didn't get that joy, you know.

key_insights.insight: Society often pressures individuals, especially women, to achieve unrealistic standards of 'having it all,' leading to exhaustion and a disconnect from their true selves.
key_insights.quote: And you just thought, oh God, how shit is that life? Where are you? Where are you? And I lost me in that. There wasn't times where it wasn't fantastic there was but where was i i didn't stop to breathe i didn't stop to truly connect truly connect with me

key_insights.insight: True joy comes from connecting with one's inner self and living in alignment with one's own truth, not conforming to societal expectations.
key_insights.quote: So the only true path to joy is to accept who you are.

key_insights.insight: Many people never realize that there is a path to self-discovery and living in alignment with their true selves, often due to societal pressures.
key_insights.quote: Some people never even know that path there. You know, most people don't.

key_insights.insight: It's essential to allow oneself and others (including employees and children) to express their truth and connect with what is genuinely important to them, rather than forcing conformity.
key_insights.quote: And I think what I've tried to do is to allow the people who work with me express that and know about it and we share it we share it in the business and it just opens this whole thing up

key_insights.insight: Living a life misaligned with one's true self leads to deep regret and pain, which can be seen even in the faces of those near death.
key_insights.quote: And he'd go in and he'd see these little pinched faces that had lived a life that wasn't in line with their true self because they couldn't, they had no choice. And he just said it used to make him feel so, so sad.

key_insights.insight: Even if one can afford the best education or opportunities for their children, it's crucial to allow them to choose their own path based on their inner truth and desires, not societal expectations.
key_insights.quote: I mean, I put them through a great academic system because I could, but I always said, you choose.

actionable_advice.advice: Take time for self-reflection to identify your true self.
actionable_advice.how_to: Schedule regular periods of solitude for journaling, meditation, or simply being present without distractions to understand your values and desires.

actionable_advice.advice: Say 'no' to commitments and activities that do not align with your values.
actionable_advice.how_to: Prioritize your activities to those that resonate with you, and remove any that don't to make time for what's really important.

actionable_advice.advice: Encourage others around you to be their authentic selves.
actionable_advice.how_to: Create safe space for expressing opinions and ideas to foster a non-judgemental environment, to promote authenticity.

memorable_stories: Mary's experience at an expensive spa where she broke down crying during a yoga session, leading her to seek guidance from an Indian guru and discover Eckhart Tolle's 'A New Earth'.
The story of the Irish poet who gave last rites and witnessed the pain leaving the faces of those who hadn't lived authentically.
Mary's daughter choosing a less lucrative path after graduating from Oxford, focusing on work that aligned with her inner truth.
contrarian_claims: That achieving material success and external validation does not necessarily lead to happiness and can, in fact, lead to profound unhappiness.
That 'having it all' as portrayed in media is a fallacy and can be detrimental to one's well-being.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 24 - Mary Portas on Finding Your Truth When You’re Feeling Lost
